---
title: "Ross Wardrup"
subtitle: "Spatial epidemiology · public-health GIS · applied statistical software"
toc: false
---

::: {.lede}
I work where public-health data, geography, and software meet — building the
pipelines that turn messy surveillance and environmental data into something
analyzable, and doing the spatial-statistics and modeling work on top of it.
:::

I'm an MPH Epidemiology student at the University of Memphis, writing a
quantitative thesis on West Nile virus mosquito surveillance in California. I
also develop GIS and risk-modeling software in support of FEMA's Hazus program,
and build open tools for spatial epidemiology in low-resource settings. The
through-line is methods: getting the spatial statistics right, treating
surveillance effort and detection bias as part of the problem, and writing
analyses someone else can reproduce.

## Selected work

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
[West Nile virus thesis](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd){.card-title}

California mosquito surveillance — modeling infection rate in mosquito pools
against environment, surveillance effort, and space-time structure.

[Spatial epidemiology]{.tag} [Bayesian modeling]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[MESA](projects/mesa.qmd){.card-title}

A C++ desktop tool for public-health mapping in sparse, rural areas — geocoding
and spatial cluster detection, validated against established libraries.

[Spatial statistics]{.tag} [C++]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[FEMA Hazus](projects/hazus.qmd){.card-title}

GIS and risk-modeling software in support of FEMA's natural-hazard
loss-estimation program: hazard workflows, data engineering, and validation.

[GIS]{.tag} [Risk modeling]{.tag} [C#/C++]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[SEUHD practicum](projects/seuhd-practicum.qmd){.card-title}

Applied public-health GIS with a health department — respiratory-illness
hot-spot analysis and immunization-compliance reporting.

[Applied epidemiology]{.tag} [ArcGIS]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag}
:::

:::

## Elsewhere

::: {.contact-strip}
**Ross Wardrup** &nbsp;·&nbsp;
[minorsecond@gmail.com](mailto:minorsecond@gmail.com) &nbsp;·&nbsp;
[github.com/minorsecond](https://github.com/minorsecond) &nbsp;·&nbsp;
[Curriculum vitae](cv.qmd)
:::
